---
canvas:
  type: new_quiz
  title: "Beam Bending Concepts"
  published: true
  shuffle_answers: true
  shuffle_questions: true
  points: 10
  allowed_attempts: -1
  score_to_keep: highest
  time_limit: 30
  omit_from_final_grade: true
  result_view:
    restricted: false
    show_questions: true
    show_student_responses: true
    show_correctness: true
    show_correct_answers: true
    show_points_awarded: true
---

:::: {.question name="Bending Stress Formula" points_possible="2.0"}

The flexure formula for the bending stress in a beam is:

- [x] $\sigma = \dfrac{M c}{I}$
  - Correct.
- [ ] $\sigma = \dfrac{T r}{J}$
  - That is the torsion formula.
- [ ] $\sigma = \dfrac{F}{A}$

::::

:::: {.question type="multiple_answers_question" name="Neutral Axis" points_possible="3.0"}

Select all true statements about the neutral axis of a symmetric beam in pure
bending.

- [x] It passes through the centroid of the cross-section.
- [x] The bending stress there is zero.
- [ ] It carries the maximum bending stress.

::::

:::: {.question type="true_false_question" name="Linear Stress Distribution" points_possible="1.0"}

In pure bending, the normal stress varies linearly with distance from the
neutral axis.

- [x] True
  - Correct — $\sigma(y) = M y / I$.
- [ ] False

::::
